title: outl plugin manifest
description: Schema for plugin.json — the contract between an outl plugin and the host. Validated at install time and on every load.

$schema.description: Optional pointer back to this schema, for editor autocomplete.

id.description: Stable reverse-DNS identity. Never changes across versions. Used as the install directory name and the op-log actor stamp (plugin:<id>@<device>).

version.description: Plugin version (semver). Resolved from the install tag and frozen in installed.json.

api.description: Plugin API range this plugin targets (semver range against the outl plugin API, NOT the binary version). A host whose plugin API major does not satisfy this range refuses to load the plugin.

main.description: Path to the bundled entry file, relative to the plugin root. A single self-contained JS file — no node_modules, no runtime resolution.

outl.description: Minimum outl binary version (semver range). Independent of `api`: this tracks the binary, `api` tracks the plugin API surface.

capabilities.description: What the plugin plugs into. The loader intersects this with what the current client implements; a capability this client cannot honor loads partially with a warning, never a crash.

permissions.description: Capabilities the plugin requests against the host. Approved on install, frozen in the lockfile; an update asking for more triggers re-approval. A bare `network:*` is rejected — network must be scoped to a domain.

anyOf.description: Network access scoped to one domain. `network:api.openai.com` (exact) or `network:*.openai.com` (leading-label wildcard). A bare `network:*` or a mid-string wildcard is rejected.

contributes.description: Static contributions the host wires up without running the plugin.

commands.description: Commands the plugin handles, surfaced in the slash menu / command palette and bindable to a chord.

id.description: Command id, unique within the plugin. The handler is registered at runtime via ctx.commands.register(id, ...).

title.description: Label shown in the slash menu / palette.

keybindings.description: Default chord bindings for the plugin's commands. They resolve through Action::PluginCommand in outl-shortcuts; the user can override them.

command.description: id of a command declared in contributes.commands.

key.description: Chord, e.g. `Ctrl+Shift+A`. (Declarable today; chord dispatch is not yet wired in any client.)

configSchema.description: Path, relative to the plugin root, to a JSON Schema file (draft 2020-12) describing this plugin's user-editable config, e.g. `config.schema.json`. The host renders a form from it and validates the value before handing it to ctx.config.get(). Day-zero accepts a path only, never an inline schema.

toolbar.description: Toolbar buttons the plugin contributes to GUI clients' chrome (capability `toolbar-button`). Tapping a button runs the referenced command.

command.description: id of a command declared in contributes.commands.

icon.description: Short glyph/emoji shown on the button.

transformers.description: Content transformers: code-fence languages the plugin renders into a descriptor each client draws (capabilities `content-transformer:text` / `:rich`).

kind.description: `text` (rendered on every client) or `rich` (HTML in a sandboxed iframe, GUI only).

metadata.description: Non-load-bearing descriptive fields. Surfaced in discovery UI and the registry.

locales.description: BCP-47 locale tags the plugin ships strings for.

category.description: Free-form category used by discovery, e.g. `productivity`, `export`.
